title: CSV load failures are silently swallowed to an empty dataset
evidence: src/soccer_data.erl:64-75 load_all_matches wraps each parse in `catch _:_ -> []`; src/soccer_data.erl:79-81 load_players likewise. A missing/malformed CSV yields zero rows with no error surfaced.
suggestion: Log or propagate parse/file errors so silent data-coverage loss is detectable rather than masked as an empty result set.

title: Acceptance tests share a suite-wide dataset rather than starting from an empty, isolated system per scenario
evidence: test/soccer_mcp_SUITE.erl:28-43 init_per_suite loads data once and all ac01..ac15 read it; the ATDD prompt asks each scenario to 'start from a running but empty system and share no data with other tests'.
suggestion: For a read-only query SUT seeded from fixed CSVs this is defensible, but per-scenario fixtures would more strictly satisfy the ATDD independence directive.

title: List queries support limit but no offset/pagination
evidence: src/soccer_tools.erl:106-112 (find_matches) and 150-156 (find_players) apply lists:sublist(Sorted, Limit) with no offset; TASK.md example answers reference paging ('15 more matches in dataset').
suggestion: Add an offset arg to find_matches/find_players for paging through large result sets.

title: Zero third-party dependencies; uses OTP json + bespoke CSV parser
evidence: rebar.config deps=[]; src/soccer_csv.erl is a hand-written quoted-field CSV parser; JSON via OTP's built-in json module.
suggestion: No action — noted as a notable, self-contained implementation choice.

title: Team stats include home/away breakdown beyond the required W/L/D and goals
evidence: src/soccer_data.erl:304-339 compute_team_stats also tracks home_wins/draws/losses and away_wins/draws/losses, win_rate, and points (R6 only required W/L/D + goals).
